text:
Hi everyone, my name is Ade. I’ve been a full stack developer for a little over 10 years. I’ve been into programming for over 20 years though.

Interested in chatting and learning more about what you guys are working on!

Interested in chatting and learning more about what you guys are working on!

text: Hello everyone! My name is Lovisa, and I'm a student in applied biostatistics at Sahlgrenska Academy, Gothenburg University, Sweden. Shortly, biostatistics is basically statistics applied on medical data, which involves a lot of programming for running various analyses on big datasets. Apart from programming as part of my masters programme, I also have some personal projects I'm working at. I code mosly in R, and have some basic knowledge in Python. The first time I tried programming was just over a year ago. Some of my data and programming interests are equality data, womens health and epidemiology overall. I look forward to be able to do maps that visualizes spread of disease (etc), to get on with GitHub and to successively become a more independent and confident programmer. Most programmers I know focuses on Python. It would be great to chat with some other R programmers!

Shortly, biostatistics is basically statistics applied on medical data, which involves a lot of programming for running various analyses on big datasets. text:
<@U013ZLJARC7> I'm humbled that you want to hear more - I'm a huge fan of your work! I'll definitely make sure to share what I do here. I am coordinating an entire 63 student studio cohort and we're doing a ground-up analysis of our curriculum so sadly my software projects are a bit slow at the moment. But I have a big one on the horizon that I'm trying to put the pieces in place for.

In the interim, an old paper you might like - in trying to "look inside" the lattices my library makes, I hit on a pretty neat method for using "section" cuts through the objects to describe the internal organization of planes and volumes...

elements.text:
I'm humbled that you want to hear more - I'm a huge fan of your work! I'll definitely make sure to share what I do here. I am coordinating an entire 63 student studio cohort and we're doing a ground-up analysis of our curriculum so sadly my software projects are a bit slow at the moment. But I have a big one on the horizon that I'm trying to put the pieces in place for.

In the interim, an old paper you might like - in trying to "look inside" the lattices my library makes, I hit on a pretty neat method for using "section" cuts through the objects to describe the internal organization of planes and volumes...
